definition: Represent each protein binding site as a local pocket geometry, atom cloud, surface, residue set, or pocket descriptor, then compare pockets by 3D alignment kernels, shape overlap, atomic-property fields, or precomputed pocket-similarity tools. For SPECTRA, this audits whether ligand-binding or function predictions generalize to pockets that are locally unlike training pockets even when global protein similarity is low.
example_usage: python metric_to_similarity.py --input pocket_scores.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --metric-kind similarity --metric-col pocket_similarity

citations.title: A new protein binding pocket similarity measure based on comparison of clouds of atoms in 3D: application to ligand prediction

evidence.summary: The paper motivates local binding-pocket comparison when proteins lack global sequence or structure similarity.
It defines pocket similarity from atom clouds and convolution kernels.
The authors state the method compares binding sites in 3D even without overall sequence or structure similarity.

citations.title: Estimating the Similarity between Protein Pockets

evidence.summary: Paperclip returned a review of computational methods for comparing protein binding sites and estimating druggability.

limitations: Pocket definitions vary across tools and can change under apo/holo conformation differences.
Local pocket similarity may disagree with sequence, global structure, or ligand chemical similarity.
Accurate pocket comparison often requires specialized software beyond the bundled generic metric converter.
quality_gates: Report pocket extraction method, ligand/holo assumptions, atom or surface features, alignment method, and score scale.
Compare pocket similarity with global protein-structure and ligand-similarity curves where possible.
Check sensitivity to apo/holo structures, pocket-radius cutoffs, and missing residues.
